resources.string: It is a widely used scale to assess static and dynamic balance in both sitting and standing positions.

Each item is scored from 0 to 4. A score of 0 indicates inability to perform the task, and a score of 4 indicates the ability to complete the task independently.\nItems are scored based on time, level of independence, or supervision required.
0 to 20: Wheelchair dependent.\n21 to 40: Able to walk with assistance.\n41 to 56: Independent.
To detect changes, it is advisable to perform the test at least twice and, if possible, under the same conditions.

string-array.name: berg_materials
string-array.item: Chair between 45 and 50 cm with armrests
Chair between 45 and 50 cm without armrests

resources.string: The Motricity Index is an ordinal method used to measure motor strength in patients with hemiparesis after a stroke.
Six items (3 for the upper limb: grip, elbow flexion, shoulder abduction; and 3 for the lower limb: ankle dorsiflexion, knee extension, and hip flexion).
A higher score indicates greater overall motor strength.
Use as part of the clinical assessment to plan rehabilitation interventions focused on motor strength.
The Trunk Control Test assesses trunk control and stability through four functional movements.
Four items, each scored 0, 12, or 25. The total score ranges from 0 to 100.
A higher total score indicates better trunk control.
The TCT can be used to guide the rehabilitation plan aimed at improving trunk control.
